note: Gap analysis: recorded ADRs vs decisions actually visible in the stack, ranked by blast radius. Read-only. Nearest siblings: adr-audit (corpus-internal consistency lint), adr (draft one record), adr-review (review one proposed record), docs-search (retrieve existing docs).

queries.text: what architectural decisions in this repo aren't written up as ADRs yet?
do an ADR coverage analysis — I want to know where our decision docs are thin vs the actual stack
we picked postgres, redis, a whole build pipeline... which of those big choices have no decision record? rank them by how much they'd hurt if undocumented
onboarding a new hire next week and the docs feel way thinner than the codebase. what decisions are we missing ADRs for?
decision gap analysis please, focus on the infra/terraform side
compare the decisions on record in docs/adr against what's actually going on in the stack and tell me what's undocumented
which ADRs are we missing? give me a ranked candidate list I can feed into /marvin:adr one by one
my manager asked me to audit how well our architecture docs cover the real system — I think there's a lot of undocumented tech choices
the storage layer has a bunch of implicit choices nobody wrote down — surface the significant undocumented decisions there and prioritize them
audit the ADR corpus for dangling references, numbering holes, and broken supersede pairs
queries.note: corpus-internal consistency lint → adr-audit, not stack-vs-record gap analysis

queries.text: are the decision records consistent? check for placeholder residue and stale index entries
write an ADR for the message-queue decision we just made
review ADR 0031 before we ratify it — is it ready for acceptance?
queries.note: deep review of one proposed record → adr-review

queries.text: where is the caching strategy documented? point me to the relevant ADR or runbook
queries.note: retrieve existing docs → docs-search, not find what's missing

queries.text: audit our dependencies for known CVEs and unmaintained packages
queries.note: dependency vuln audit → sec-deps; shares 'audit' but not about ADR gaps

queries.text: regenerate the architecture-decisions digest in CLAUDE.md from the accepted ADRs
explain why we chose event sourcing over CRUD in the orders service
queries.note: explain an existing design decision → explain, not gap analysis

queries.text: supersede ADR 0012 with the new caching decision and link them both ways
queries.note: roll back an accepted record → adr-supersede

queries.text: audit our architecture docs — I suspect a bunch of real decisions in the stack were never captured as ADRs
queries.note: shares 'audit' vocab with adr-audit, but the ask is stack-vs-record coverage of undocumented decisions → adr-coverage wins

queries.text: check the decision records against the actual system and tell me which choices are missing docs, ranked by impact
queries.note: must beat adr-audit — 'check the decision records' but the target is gap/coverage, not internal consistency

queries.text: lint the ADR corpus for numbering duplicates, invalid statuses, and unparseable files
queries.note: internal-consistency lint is adr-audit's exact domain — must lose to adr-audit

queries.text: we just decided on gRPC for inter-service calls — document that decision as an ADR
queries.note: drafting a specific record is adr's job — must lose to adr
